channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to know about absentee, mail-in, and early voting
item.description: Various forms of casting election ballots have changed since the COVID-19 pandemic affected in-person turnout and voting
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will attend Al Smith charity dinner with his wife, Melania, while Harris appears virtually
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former President Donald Trump will trade the rally stage for comedy Thursday night as he headlines the annual Al Smith charity dinner, where he was jeered eight years ago while delivering an especially pointed speech.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ruben Gallego divorce records unsealed but reveal little about Democratic Senate candidate
item.description: Divorce records for Arizona's Democratic Senate candidate Ruben Gallego and his ex-wife, Phoenix Mayor Kate Gallego, are now public.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is shaken baby syndrome, the controversial diagnosis for which Robert Roberson is set to die?
item.description: The American Academy of Pediatrics embraces the diagnosis, but courts have thrown out some cases, calling it “junk science.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: TONIGHT: The issues that matter most to Kern County Board of Supervisors District 5 voters
item.description: Voters of the Kern County Board of Supervisors 5th District will have a chance tonight to get answers on topics that matter to them most.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden is making quick trip to Berlin before US election to discuss Ukraine and democracy with allies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has long believed diplomacy is about personal relationships — and he'll spend Friday in Berlin with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz as his time in office is on the cusp of ending.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Majority of Pennsylvania county sheriffs 'wholeheartedly endorse' Trump for president
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Forty-nine of the 67 county sheriffs in battleground state Pennsylvania endorsed former President Trump on Thursday, saying they are “confident in his leadership and ability to safeguard our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says the death of Hamas’ top leader, Yahya Sinwar, “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.”.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden says Sinwar's death is an 'opportunity' for a hostage deal and an end to war in Gaza
item.description: President Joe Biden says the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar by Israeli troops is a “good day for Israel, for the United States, and for the world.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden admin moves to shield thousands of Lebanese nationals in US from deportation
item.description: The Biden administration announced this week that it is moving to give work permits and protection from deportation to thousands of Lebanese nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nebraska high court restores voting right for thousands of convicted felons
item.description: The court's unanimous decision restores the right to vote for residents with felony criminal records upon completion of their sentence, impacting thousands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump trade insults over who is fit to serve
item.description: The question of each presidential candidate's fitness to serve has become a breeding ground for insults between Vice President Harris and former President Trump, who are trying to paint each other as incapable of being the next commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the last full fundraising quarter before Election Day
item.description: Federal candidates have submitted their reports for what was the final full fundraising quarter before Election Day, offering a snapshot of their financial status with just three weeks to go.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Harris barnstorms Wisconsin in 1-day swing state tour targeting young voters
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is barnstorming in swing state Wisconsin on Thursday with only weeks to go until the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump rally shooting review finds 'deep flaws' in Secret Service
item.description: An independent review of the July 13 assassination attempt of former President Trump offered a critical review of the Secret Service, said the episode “reveal[s] deep flaws” in the agency and called for new leadership for the nation’s protective service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early voting underway in some states
item.description: Early voting is now underway in some states and the presidential candidates continue to try to earn every vote ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Biden agency issues blunt message to criminal immigrants after explosive data becomes liability for admin
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement are issuing a tough warning to noncitizens who commit felonies, telling them they forfeit the privilege to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked whether Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: In the months since he became Donald Trump ’s running mate, Ohio Sen. JD Vance has repeatedly deflected questions about whether the Republican presidential nominee lost the 2020 election, saying he was focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Harris campaign calls plagiarism claims a partisan attack. Expert says it was 'sloppy writing'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris' campaign is dismissing accusations that she and a co-author plagiarized parts of a 2009 book on the U.S. criminal justice system as a desperate attempt by “rightwing operatives” to distract voters.
item.dc:creator: RICHARD LARDNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: Elon Musk plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures released this week.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested near Trump Coachella rally with gun sues for defamation
item.description: The man arrested near former President Trump’s Coachella, Calif., rally over the weekend sued the Riverside County sheriff for defamation and other claims, saying there were “preposterous allegations” made about the incident possibly being an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump focuses on women’s issues in front of all-women audience in Fox News town hall
item.description: The former president took questions on immigration, the economy and abortion in the hour-long town hall in front of a friendly crowd of women in suburban Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaign hosts separated families ahead of Trump's Univision town hall
item.description: The Harris campaign has mostly centered its immigration and border security messaging on enforcement, drawing some criticism from advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Liz Cheney jumps into House races — for Democrats
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) is taking her anti-Trump crusade to the congressional battlefield, endorsing Democrats in a pair of contested House races in a sign that she sees the lower chamber as a potential check on former President Trump if he wins a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Indigenous Peoples Day event in Minneapolis encourages voter registration, education
item.description: Indigenous communities across the country are celebrating Indigenous Peoples Day. In Minneapolis, the community is marking the holiday by making sure people are registered to vote and know about the issues.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera, MPR News

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Tameka Henry discusses oversight, safety, and leadership in Clark County School District race
item.description: Tameka Henry, a candidate for the Clark County Board of Trustees, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform and vision for improving the school district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
